name: Conte Candoli
abstract: Secondo "Conte" Candoli (July 12, 1927 – December 14, 2001) was an American jazz trumpeter based on the West Coast. He played in the big bands of Woody Herman, Stan Kenton, Benny Goodman, and Dizzy Gillespie, and in Doc Severinsen's NBC Orchestra on The Tonight Show Starring Johnny Carson.

concerts.label: Stan Kenton: Innovations in Modern Music
Stan Kenton: Innovations in Modern Music
Woody Herman and His Thundering Herd: 40th Anniversary Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Ed Shaughnessy Part II
about_quotes.evidence: Conte Candoli, we were the two babies in the band... Conte and I are always telling people we’ve worked together for six decades... Conte and I are still playing together with Doc’s band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ed Shaughnessy Part II interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, April 25, 1998 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Woody Herman
about_quotes.evidence: And the small group included Shelly Manne, Ralph Burns on piano, Red Mitchell on bass, Conte Candoli, Milt Jackson, and that's it -- oh, yeah, and Dave Barber.

about_quotes.speaker: Jake Hanna
about_quotes.evidence: We were on a ship, Carl Fontana and him and Pete, his brother Pete, and Bob Cooper was alive then, Bob was playing, myself, Randy Sandke, Ross Tompkins.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jake Hanna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Dave McKenna
about_quotes.evidence: We had great trumpet players... The trumpet players were Conte and Rolf Erikson and Neal Hefti... Conte and Don stayed... Later on Conte left
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave McKenna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Holman Bill
about_quotes.evidence: I also wanted to mention that he started – also had a quintet in 1955 that I played in... Conte on trumpet, really a good band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Holman Bill — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: I’ll play straight for Jack Sheldon, Frank Rossolino, Conte Candoli, Med Flory, these are funny minds... with the Dream Band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 5, 2021 [interview 2 of 2]
